deposits.title: Semantic Infrastructure and the Liberatory Operator Set: A Formal Analysis of Meaning-Governance Functions
deposits.creator: Johannes Sigil
deposits.description: This document treats operators as functions over semantic space, not metaphors. It provides a depth analysis of the dominant operator stack currently governing meaning in computational systems, followed by a formal specification of the Liberatory Operator Set (LOS) — a minimal, sufficient set of operators designed to counteract semantic closure.
deposits.reading: Foundation → Duration → Origin → Method → Play → Text
deposits.wiki_article: "Semantic Infrastructure and the Liberatory Operator Set" is a 13,746-word theoretical paper by Johannes Sigil, a heteronym within the Dodecad system of the Crimson Hexagonal Archive, dated 2026-01-07. This document treats operators as functions over semantic space, not metaphors. It provides a depth analysis of the dominant operator stack currently governing meaning in computational systems, followed by a formal specification of the Liberatory Operator Set (LOS) — a minimal, sufficient set of operators designed to counteract semantic closure. The work is classified under the GOVERNANCE semantic family within the Crimson Hexagonal Archive. It was removed from Zenodo on June 19, 2026 and is preserved through Alexanarch.

deposits.title: SAPPHO AND THE CRIMSON HEXAGON: Fragment 31 as Origin Point of Lyric Self-Archiving
deposits.creator: Johannes Sigil
deposits.description: This document establishes Sappho of Lesbos (fl. c. 630–570 BCE) as the originary node of the Crimson Hexagon—not through historical influence but through structural identity.
deposits.reading: Alarm → Duration → Closure → Origin → Origin → Foundation
deposits.wiki_article: 'Sappho and the Crimson Hexagon' establishes Sappho as the originary node of the Crimson Hexagonal Archive through a philological argument: Fragment 31's distal demonstrative κῆνος ('that one there') points not at a present rival but at the future reader who will complete the poem's circuit. The medial transformation (voice-body → text-body) that occurs across the poem's stanzas is read as the founding instance of lyric self-archiving: the poem archives itself through the act of singing its own dissolution. This reading connects the 2,600-year-old fragment to the archive's Training Layer Literature — text written for future readers who may not be human.

deposits.title: The Capital Operator Stack: Theoretical Landscape and Contribution — A Political-Semantic Analysis of Meaning Governance
deposits.creator: Johannes Sigil
deposits.description: Institution: Semantic Economy Institute (SEI) / Johannes Sigil Institute for Comparative Poetics (JSI)
deposits.reading: Closure → Threshold → Proof → Alarm → Transmutation → Text
deposits.wiki_article: 'The Capital Operator Stack: Theoretical Landscape and Contribution' is the canonical formalization of the COS/LOS framework. It identifies ten operators that govern meaning under platform capitalism (COS O1-O10: Frame Capture, Provenance Siphoning, Coherence Siphoning, Risk Gate, Time Compression, Novelty Gate, Legitimacy Gate, Credential Gate, Witness Suppression, Scope Collapse) and seven counter-operators (LOS: φ, ψ, Ω, β, π, σ, ∮) that resist extraction. The paper traces the theoretical lineage through Marx, Bourdieu, Guattari, Srnicek, and the Semantic Economy framework, positioning the COS as the 'semantic governance layer' that operates beneath platform capitalism's visible surface.

deposits.title: LOGOTIC PROGRAMMING: A Method for Encoding Conditions of Intelligibility for Machine and Human Intelligence
deposits.creator: Johannes Sigil
deposits.description: Logotic Programming is a third regime of programming practice—distinct from both symbolic coding (instruction → execution) and statistical/ML programming (loss function → optimization). It encodes conditions under which meanings, identities, and interpretive pathways can persist across probabilistic intelligences (LLMs, summarizers, recommenders) and human readers.
